manifest.title: Engine Lab
manifest.summary: Cross-check core, SDK, CLI, API, adapter, and MCP behavior against the same bundle.

scenarios.summary: Engine package parity holds across direct, SDK, CLI, API, adapter, and MCP entrypoints.

assertions.label: Receipt parity
assertions.detail: All engine receipts resolved to hash f4bb1f0cb1d00604358eb6d4f67c0c4bb2636a39006e635bbc2f121cc83f025f.

findings: @uvrn/api works for delta routes without modification.
@uvrn/embed needs an additional status route outside the official API package.

manifest.title: Ingestion Lab
manifest.summary: Fetch mock provider data over HTTP and run farm, normalize, consensus, and score flows.

scenarios.summary: Mock provider endpoints behave like real ingestion points and flow cleanly through farm, normalize, consensus, and score.

consensusStats.summary: Consensus derived from 4 usable sources with agreement 25.0, coverage 100.0, recency 97.6, and weighted consensus 93.3.

scoreBreakdown.explanation: V-Score: 87.5 (financial profile) — Completeness: 88/100 (30.8 pts) — Parity: 90/100 (31.5 pts) — Freshness: 84/100 (25.2 pts) — Consensus is STABLE. — Freshness is the limiting factor.

findings: ConsensusEngine depends on numeric tokens being present in provider snippets or titles.
Demo-owned connectors provide a clean zero-external path without touching package reference connectors.

manifest.title: Lifecycle Lab
manifest.summary: Drive drift, agent, signal, watch, canon, and timeline from mock-ingested claim changes.

scenarios.summary: Lifecycle packages work together when the claim path is driven by HTTP-backed mock connectors and in-process stores.

claims.label: SOL momentum remains stable through the monitoring window

alertEvents.summary: Claim clm_sol_momentum_001 entered DRIFTING status at V-Score 71.5 (delta -13.6) at 2026-04-03T01:10:37.559Z.
Claim clm_sol_momentum_001 entered CRITICAL status at V-Score 54.3 (delta -17.2) at 2026-04-03T01:10:37.627Z.

triggered_by.reason: Demo promotion after stable first run

timeline.summary: Claim clm_sol_momentum_001 has no timeline snapshots in the requested range.

logs: Lifecycle lab used http://127.0.0.1:4175 for farm ingestion.
findings: The zero-external path is strong when watch delivery stays on callback mode and canon uses mock signer/store.
Timeline package already supports both store mode and API mode, which makes it easy to bridge into the dashboard.

manifest.title: Analysis Lab
manifest.summary: Compare claim histories, score signer reputation, and use @uvrn/test fixtures as setup accelerators.

scenarios.summary: Analysis packages are strongest when fed by generated scenario history and mock stores rather than external systems.

compare.summary: Claim clm_eth_treasury_001 leads with V-Score 91.2 vs clm_sol_momentum_001 at 44.1 for a delta of 47.1. Claims diverged 2026-03-29T12:00:00.000Z.

series.summary: Claim clm_sol_momentum_001 is declining with latest V-Score 44.1, peak 92.1, and average change -24.00 per period.

logs: CompareEngine consumed receipt-like series objects directly.
IdentityRegistry used MockIdentityStore for reputation scoring.
findings: @uvrn/test is valuable as scaffolding even outside unit tests.
CompareEngine tolerates mixed receipt-like object shapes, which makes demo wiring easier.

manifest.title: UI Lab
manifest.summary: Render @uvrn/embed against the local claim status route and document the plain embed path.

scenarios.summary: UI integration stays simple once the local demo API satisfies the embed package status contract.

assertions.label: Plain embed snippet available
assertions.detail: The demo ships both React and plain HTML embed examples.

outputs.note: The dashboard renders the live React badge. The plain HTML snippet is documented for external consumers.

findings: @uvrn/embed remains lightweight when pointed at a compatible local status route.

findings.notes: Core runs cleanly as the deterministic base for bundle validation, execution, and receipt verification.
evidence: Engine Lab parity checks matched across local, CLI, and HTTP flows.

findings.notes: The SDK works well in local and HTTP modes and is easiest to validate against the same bundle fixture.
evidence: Engine Lab local and HTTP receipts matched the core receipt hash.

findings.notes: CLI smoke runs are straightforward once the package is built and the bundle is written to disk.
evidence: Engine Lab CLI JSON output matched the core receipt hash.

findings.notes: Official delta routes work as-is. The demo adds claim status and timeline routes because those are outside the current package surface.
evidence: Engine Lab HTTP run, validate, and verify routes returned expected results.

findings.notes: The MCP server is smoke-tested over stdio by listing tools and calling delta_run_engine against the shared bundle.
evidence: Engine Lab MCP smoke completed with tool listing and tool execution.

findings.notes: Adapter wrapping works cleanly around core receipts with a local random wallet and no network requirements.
evidence: Engine Lab DRVC3 wrapping preserved the embedded delta receipt hash.

findings.notes: Drift snapshots and threshold events are easy to drive once mock-ingested evidence is converted into agent scores.
evidence: Lifecycle Lab crossed STABLE -> DRIFTING -> CRITICAL.

findings.notes: Agent integrations are strongest when farm data arrives through a real connector boundary rather than direct fixtures.
evidence: Lifecycle Lab used HTTP-backed connectors and emitted threshold events.

findings.notes: Manual canonization works well with mock signer and store implementations from @uvrn/test.
evidence: Lifecycle Lab produced a canon receipt and storage proof.

findings.notes: SignalBus is lightweight and easy to bridge from agent and watch events in-process.
evidence: Lifecycle Lab emitted agent, drift, canon, and watch signals.

findings.notes: ScoreBreakdown is useful as the explanation layer for ingestion and lifecycle outputs.
evidence: Ingestion Lab produced a score breakdown from normalized components.

findings.notes: The package is best used as a setup accelerator for demos, tests, and mock signer/store support.
evidence: Analysis Lab consumed fixtures and mock signer/store helpers.

findings.notes: Farm is the clearest place to test provider-style boundaries. Demo-owned connectors keep the default path zero-external.
evidence: Ingestion Lab fetched mock provider data over local HTTP through BaseConnector-based adapters.

findings.notes: Normalization fits naturally between farm ingestion and consensus bundle construction.
evidence: Ingestion Lab normalized HTTP-fetched FarmSource objects into stable output.

findings.notes: ConsensusEngine is effective once mock provider snippets carry usable numeric tokens.
evidence: Ingestion Lab built a DeltaBundle and consensus stats from mock-ingested evidence.

findings.notes: CompareEngine works well on mixed receipt-like shapes and benefits from timeline-aligned data.
evidence: Analysis Lab compared two claim histories and generated a divergence summary.

findings.notes: IdentityRegistry is straightforward to demo with MockIdentityStore and recorded activities.
evidence: Analysis Lab produced leaderboard-ready reputation output.

findings.notes: Timeline works locally with MockTimelineStore and can also target a remote API if a compatible route exists.
evidence: Lifecycle Lab returned sampled snapshots and chart output.

findings.notes: The in-process callback path is the best default for demos. Third-party delivery targets stay optional.
evidence: Lifecycle Lab delivered callback alerts without external services.

findings.notes: Embed renders cleanly against a local claim status endpoint. The demo API provides the route expected by the package.
evidence: UI Lab renders the live badge against /claims/:claimId/status.
